clusters.title: U.S. and Saudi Arabia sign nuclear cooperation deal
clusters.neutral_headline: US signs landmark nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia
bullet_summary: Department of Energy announced Wednesday
The Trump administration announced a landmark agreement with Saudi Arabia on Wednesday that allows the Middle Eastern country to develop a civilian nuclear program on its soil
Washington said the deal includes strict non-proliferation safeguards as regional tensions continue to escalate

items.title: U.S. and Saudi Arabia sign nuclear cooperation deal
items.standfirst: The U.S. and Saudi Arabia signed a deal for U.S. companies to run nuclear power on Saudi soil, the U.S. Department of Energy announced Wednesday.
items.content: The U.S. and Saudi Arabia signed a deal for U.S. companies to run nuclear power on Saudi soil, the U.S. Department of Energy announced Wednesday.

items.title: US signs landmark nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia
items.standfirst: The US Department of Energy says the "peaceful" co-operation agreement will give US firms "great access" to the Saudi nuclear energy programme.
items.content: The US Department of Energy says the "peaceful" co-operation agreement will give US firms "great access" to the Saudi nuclear energy programme.

items.title: Watch: What we know about the US-Saudi nuclear deal
items.standfirst: The BBC's Sarah Smith explains the implications of the landmark cooperation agreement between both countries.
items.content: The BBC's Sarah Smith explains the implications of the landmark cooperation agreement between both countries.

items.title: US and Saudi Arabia agree landmark nuclear energy pact
items.standfirst: Critics warn agreement lacks safeguards to prevent future uranium enrichment and reprocessing
items.content: Critics warn agreement lacks safeguards to prevent future uranium enrichment and reprocessing

items.title: Trump administration officially announces nuclear cooperation agreement with Saudi Arabia
items.standfirst: The Trump administration announced a landmark agreement with Saudi Arabia on Wednesday that allows the Middle Eastern country to develop a civilian nuclear program on its soil. CBS News senior White House correspondent Weijia Jiang has the details.
items.content: The Trump administration announced a landmark agreement with Saudi Arabia on Wednesday that allows the Middle Eastern country to develop a civilian nuclear program on its soil. CBS News senior White House correspondent Weijia Jiang has the details.

items.title: US and Saudi Arabia announce nuclear cooperation deal
items.standfirst: Trump administration says 'historic' agreement will 'reinforce global nonproliferation standards' and create jobs in US.
items.content: Trump administration says 'historic' agreement will 'reinforce global nonproliferation standards' and create jobs in US.

items.title: US announces nuclear cooperation pact with Saudi Arabia
items.standfirst: The United States on Wednesday announced that it had reached a civilian nuclear cooperation deal with Saudi Arabia, with Energy Secretary Chris Wright signing the pact and a separate bilateral safeguards deal alongside Saudi Energy Minister Prince Abdulaziz bin Salman. FRANCE 24's Fraser Jackson reports from Washington.
items.content: The United States on Wednesday announced that it had reached a civilian nuclear cooperation deal with Saudi Arabia, with Energy Secretary Chris Wright signing the pact and a separate bilateral safeguards deal alongside Saudi Energy Minister Prince Abdulaziz bin Salman. FRANCE 24's Fraser Jackson reports from Washington.

items.title: Trump administration signs commercial nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia
items.standfirst: The agreement gives American companies priority access to nuclear reactors and fuel to Saudi Arabia. It's expected to last decades and be worth billions of dollars.
items.content: The agreement gives American companies priority access to nuclear reactors and fuel to Saudi Arabia. It's expected to last decades and be worth billions of dollars.(Image credit: Andrew Harnik)

items.title: US announces landmark deal allowing Saudis to enrich nuclear fuel
items.standfirst: The United States on Wednesday signed a landmark civilian nuclear agreement with Saudi Arabia, pressing ahead with closer strategic ties despite the war with Iran. Washington said the deal includes strict non-proliferation safeguards as regional tensions continue to escalate.
items.content: The United States on Wednesday signed a landmark civilian nuclear agreement with Saudi Arabia, pressing ahead with closer strategic ties despite the war with Iran. Washington said the deal includes strict non-proliferation safeguards as regional tensions continue to escalate.

items.title: Trump to announce US-Saudi nuclear deal to allow fuel enrichment
items.standfirst: The New York Times is reporting that the Trump administration plans to announce that it has reached a broad nuclear agreement with Saudi Arabia which could lead to the country enriching its own fuel for nuclear reactors.What do we know about the deal and what will this mean for the region? Details by Angela Diffley, FRANCE 24 international affairs editor.
items.content: The New York Times is reporting that the Trump administration plans to announce that it has reached a broad nuclear agreement with Saudi Arabia which could lead to the country enriching its own fuel for nuclear reactors.What do we know about the deal and what will this mean for the region? Details by Angela Diffley, FRANCE 24 international affairs editor.

items.title: US signs nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia
items.standfirst: The US and Saudi Arabia have reached a nuclear cooperation deal.
items.content: The US and Saudi Arabia have reached a nuclear cooperation deal.

items.title: U.S. signs landmark nuclear agreement with Saudi Arabia
items.standfirst: The U.S. will support creating a civilian nuclear power program in the kingdom under the agreement, a senior administration official told NBC News.
items.content: The U.S. will support creating a civilian nuclear power program in the kingdom under the agreement, a senior administration official told NBC News.

items.title: U.S. launches more Iran strikes as threats to oil trade deepen
items.standfirst: President Trump threatened to bomb an Iranian bridge or power plant for every ship attacked in the Strait of Hormuz, as one shipping insider says "nothing is going through."
items.content: President Trump threatened to bomb an Iranian bridge or power plant for every ship attacked in the Strait of Hormuz, as one shipping insider says "nothing is going through."

items.title: Trump threatens to target Iran's bridges and power plants if Hormuz attacks persist
items.standfirst: Donald Trump says the US will respond any time Iranian forces shoot at a ship in the Strait of Hormuz.
items.content: Donald Trump says the US will respond any time Iranian forces shoot at a ship in the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Trump and Iran trade threats to attack energy infrastructure as U.S. completes 11th night of strikes
items.standfirst: President Trump on Wednesday said that for every Iranian drone or missile attack in the Strait of Hormuz, the U.S. will "bomb and destroy one bridge or power plant." Iran said if the U.S. hits civilian targets it will seek to halt all oil flow from the region. CBS News' Charlie D'Agata and Samantha Vinograd have the latest.
items.content: President Trump on Wednesday said that for every Iranian drone or missile attack in the Strait of Hormuz, the U.S. will "bomb and destroy one bridge or power plant." Iran said if the U.S. hits civilian targets it will seek to halt all oil flow from the region. CBS News' Charlie D'Agata and Samantha Vinograd have the latest.

items.title: Trump threatens Iranian infrastructure over Strait of Hormuz attacks
items.standfirst: US President Donald Trump warned on Wednesday that the US would destroy an Iranian bridge or power plant every time Tehran attacked shipping in the Strait of Hormuz. The threat came as Washington and Iran exchanged fresh strikes, regional missile alerts sounded and diplomatic efforts remained stalled.
items.content: US President Donald Trump warned on Wednesday that the US would destroy an Iranian bridge or power plant every time Tehran attacked shipping in the Strait of Hormuz. The threat came as Washington and Iran exchanged fresh strikes, regional missile alerts sounded and diplomatic efforts remained stalled.

clusters.title: Ukrainians welcome new army chief but seek return of fired defence minister
clusters.neutral_headline: Zelenskyy under growing pressure to reinstate sacked defence minister
bullet_summary: Reported by France24, Guardian World, BBC World and others

items.title: Ukrainians welcome new army chief but seek return of fired defence minister
items.standfirst: Ukraine's soldiers and civilians welcomed the appointment of Mykhailo Drapaty as the new army chief on Wednesday, but many said they still wanted former defence minister Mykhailo Fedorov restored after his surprise removal sparked the largest war-related protests since Russia's 2022 invasion.
items.content: Ukraine's soldiers and civilians welcomed the appointment of Mykhailo Drapaty as the new army chief on Wednesday, but many said they still wanted former defence minister Mykhailo Fedorov restored after his surprise removal sparked the largest war-related protests since Russia's 2022 invasion.

items.title: Zelenskyy under growing pressure to reinstate sacked defence minister
items.standfirst: Campaigners in Ukraine pledge to continue nationwide protests until Mykhailo Fedorov gets his job back Volodymyr Zelenskyy was under growing pressure to reinstate his sacked defence minister on Wednesday, after campaigners said they would continue indefinite nationwide protests until Mykhailo Fedorov got his job back.On Tuesday, Zelenskyy bowed to public demands by firing Ukraine’s commander-in-chief Oleksandr Syrskyi. He appointed Mykhailo Drapatyi as the new head of the country’s armed forces, as part of a radical reshuffle of the top military command. Continue reading...
items.content: Campaigners in Ukraine pledge to continue nationwide protests until Mykhailo Fedorov gets his job back Volodymyr Zelenskyy was under growing pressure to reinstate his sacked defence minister on Wednesday, after campaigners said they would continue indefinite nationwide protests until Mykhailo Fedorov got his job back.On Tuesday, Zelenskyy bowed to public demands by firing Ukraine’s commander-in-chief Oleksandr Syrskyi. He appointed Mykhailo Drapatyi as the new head of the country’s armed forces, as part of a radical reshuffle of the top military command. Continue reading...

items.title: Who is Mikhailo Drapaty, Ukraine's new commander-in-chief?
items.standfirst: Volodymyr Zelensky has bowed to pressure from the streets. The former head of the Ukrainian armed forces Oleksandr Syrsky has been replaced by the popular Mykhailo Drapaty. Who is he?
items.content: Volodymyr Zelensky has bowed to pressure from the streets. The former head of the Ukrainian armed forces Oleksandr Syrsky has been replaced by the popular Mykhailo Drapaty. Who is he?

items.content: Oleksandr Syrskyi is dismissed after backlash over the removal of popular Defence Minister Mykhailo Fedorov.

An autopsy will be carried out ​to ‌determine the cause of death, the Nanterre prosecutor's ⁠office said
A modelling scout linked to Jeffrey Epstein has been found dead at his home in Paris

items.title: Modelling scout linked to Epstein found dead in Paris
items.standfirst: Daniel Siad, whose name appears in the Epstein files thousands of times, denied knowledge of the threat Jeffrey Epstein posed.
items.content: Daniel Siad, whose name appears in the Epstein files thousands of times, denied knowledge of the threat Jeffrey Epstein posed.

items.title: French modelling scout linked to Epstein found dead
items.standfirst: Daniel Siad, ⁠a modelling scout with ​ties to the late convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein, has been ​found dead at his home outside Paris, prosecutors said on Wednesday. Siad's body was discovered on Monday evening. An autopsy will be carried out ​to ‌determine the cause of death, the Nanterre prosecutor's ⁠office said. Siad's name appeared nearly 2,000 times in the Epstein files released by the ‌U.S. Department of Justice. FRANCE 24's Emerald Maxwell reports.
items.content: Daniel Siad, ⁠a modelling scout with ​ties to the late convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein, has been ​found dead at his home outside Paris, prosecutors said on Wednesday. Siad's body was discovered on Monday evening. An autopsy will be carried out ​to ‌determine the cause of death, the Nanterre prosecutor's ⁠office said. Siad's name appeared nearly 2,000 times in the Epstein files released by the ‌U.S. Department of Justice. FRANCE 24's Emerald Maxwell reports.

items.title: Modelling scout linked to Epstein found dead at Paris home

items.content: A modelling scout linked to Jeffrey Epstein has been found dead at his home in Paris.

Reported by BBC World, Guardian World, France24

items.title: Mamdani backs off pledge to arrest Netanyahu citing lack of authority
items.standfirst: The New York City mayor instead called on US authorities to act on International Criminal Court's arrest warrant against the Israeli prime minister.
items.content: The New York City mayor instead called on US authorities to act on International Criminal Court's arrest warrant against the Israeli prime minister.

items.title: Mamdani says he can’t arrest Netanyahu as mayor and urges US to enforce ICC warrant
items.standfirst: Mamdani admits he lacks power to arrest Israeli prime minister after saying he’s ‘not welcome’ in New York CityZohran Mamdani, the New York City mayor, doubled down on his support for the international criminal court (ICC) on Wednesday and called on federal authorities to execute the court’s warrant for Benjamin Netanyahu’s arrest should he visit the city in the fall.“I call on the federal government to join the international criminal court and to execute both this warrant as well as any warrant the international criminal court holds,” Mamdani told reporters at a press conference on Wednesday. Continue reading...
items.content: Mamdani admits he lacks power to arrest Israeli prime minister after saying he’s ‘not welcome’ in New York CityZohran Mamdani, the New York City mayor, doubled down on his support for the international criminal court (ICC) on Wednesday and called on federal authorities to execute the court’s warrant for Benjamin Netanyahu’s arrest should he visit the city in the fall.“I call on the federal government to join the international criminal court and to execute both this warrant as well as any warrant the international criminal court holds,” Mamdani told reporters at a press conference on Wednesday. Continue reading...

items.title: #Gaza: #Mamdani urges government to arrest #Netanyahu if he visits #US
items.standfirst: In a video posted to social media on July 21, #NYC mayor Zohran #Mamdani urges the US federal government to arrest Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin #Netanyahu if he visits the #US later this year, after admitting he does not have the authority to enforce the #ICC's arrest warrant.
items.content: In a video posted to social media on July 21, #NYC mayor Zohran #Mamdani urges the US federal government to arrest Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin #Netanyahu if he visits the #US later this year, after admitting he does not have the authority to enforce the #ICC's arrest warrant.

items.title: Mamdani urges US government to arrest Netanyahu if Israeli PM visits US
items.standfirst: New York mayor admits he does not have authority to arrest Netanyahu, adding Israeli PM is ‘architect of a horrific genocide’Zohran Mamdani, the New York City mayor, acknowledged on Tuesday he does not have the authority to arrest Benjamin Netanyahu, but urged US federal authorities to do so if the Israeli prime minister visits the US later this year.“Benjamin Netanyahu is a war criminal, the architect of a horrific genocide against the Palestinian people,” Mamdani said in a video posted to social media. Continue reading...
items.content: New York mayor admits he does not have authority to arrest Netanyahu, adding Israeli PM is ‘architect of a horrific genocide’Zohran Mamdani, the New York City mayor, acknowledged on Tuesday he does not have the authority to arrest Benjamin Netanyahu, but urged US federal authorities to do so if the Israeli prime minister visits the US later this year.“Benjamin Netanyahu is a war criminal, the architect of a horrific genocide against the Palestinian people,” Mamdani said in a video posted to social media. Continue reading...

clusters.title: France recorded 5,700 more deaths than usual during its historic June heat wave
clusters.neutral_headline: France records more than 5,700 excess deaths during June heatwave
bullet_summary: The French national health agency said on Wednesday that it had recorded 5,764 excess deaths ⁠between ​17 June and 2 July, an excess mortality rate of 36%
France recorded 5,764 more deaths than the normal rate between June 17 and July 2, health authorities said Wednesday
While authorities said the excess mortality rate was due to "all causes", the period coincided with a stretch of extreme heatwaves in several French regions

items.title: France recorded 5,700 more deaths than usual during its historic June heat wave
items.standfirst: France recorded at least 5,700 more deaths than usual during a historic heatwave last month, the public health agency said Wednesday, sharply increasing its estimates of the feared toll from record-shattering temperatures. FRANCE 24's James Vasina reports.
items.content: France recorded at least 5,700 more deaths than usual during a historic heatwave last month, the public health agency said Wednesday, sharply increasing its estimates of the feared toll from record-shattering temperatures. FRANCE 24's James Vasina reports.

items.title: France records more than 5,700 excess deaths during June heatwave
items.standfirst: Figures come as large parts of southern Europe endure another record-breaking period of extreme temperaturesFrance recorded more than 5,700 excess deaths during its June heatwave, authorities have said, as swathes of southern Europe continue to endure another record-breaking period of extreme temperatures.The French national health agency said on Wednesday that it had recorded 5,764 excess deaths ⁠between ​17 June and 2 July, an excess mortality rate of 36%. Continue reading...
items.content: Figures come as large parts of southern Europe endure another record-breaking period of extreme temperaturesFrance recorded more than 5,700 excess deaths during its June heatwave, authorities have said, as swathes of southern Europe continue to endure another record-breaking period of extreme temperatures.The French national health agency said on Wednesday that it had recorded 5,764 excess deaths ⁠between ​17 June and 2 July, an excess mortality rate of 36%. Continue reading...

items.title: 'Unprecedented': France records 5,764 excess deaths during June-July heatwave period
items.standfirst: France recorded 5,764 more deaths than the normal rate between June 17 and July 2, health authorities said Wednesday. While authorities said the excess mortality rate was due to "all causes", the period coincided with a stretch of extreme heatwaves in several French regions.
items.content: France recorded 5,764 more deaths than the normal rate between June 17 and July 2, health authorities said Wednesday. While authorities said the excess mortality rate was due to "all causes", the period coincided with a stretch of extreme heatwaves in several French regions.

clusters.title: Canada cancels joint bridge-opening celebration with US amid trade tensions
clusters.neutral_headline: Canada cancels joint bridge-opening celebration with US amid trade tensions

The tariffs will hit a wide range of products, the White House said, including wine, hockey sticks and cement

items.title: Canada cancels joint bridge-opening celebration with US amid trade tensions
items.standfirst: Trump’s 50% tariff threat means there will be no ribbon-cutting celebration of Gordie Howe International BridgeA planned joint US-Canada ceremony on Friday celebrating the opening of the Gordie Howe International Bridge connecting Detroit and Windsor, Ontario, is no longer expected to take place after Donald Trump announced a 50% tariff on most Canadian goods.In announcing the tariffs, the president accused Canada of unfairly discriminating against US automobiles, alcohol and dairy products, escalating tensions with one of the United States’ closest allies. Continue reading...
items.content: Trump’s 50% tariff threat means there will be no ribbon-cutting celebration of Gordie Howe International BridgeA planned joint US-Canada ceremony on Friday celebrating the opening of the Gordie Howe International Bridge connecting Detroit and Windsor, Ontario, is no longer expected to take place after Donald Trump announced a 50% tariff on most Canadian goods.In announcing the tariffs, the president accused Canada of unfairly discriminating against US automobiles, alcohol and dairy products, escalating tensions with one of the United States’ closest allies. Continue reading...

items.title: Trump defends 50% tariffs on Canada, saying ‘they need us to survive’
items.standfirst: Mark Carney says Canada is ‘united’ in face of threats as leaders agree to talk before tariffs take effect next monthSign up for the Breaking News US newsletter emailDonald Trump has defended his new 50% tariffs on most Canadian goods, claiming the country cannot survive without the US.The White House announced the sweeping round of import duties – due to come into effect next month – on Monday, igniting fresh tensions between Washington and Ottawa. Continue reading...
items.content: Mark Carney says Canada is ‘united’ in face of threats as leaders agree to talk before tariffs take effect next monthSign up for the Breaking News US newsletter emailDonald Trump has defended his new 50% tariffs on most Canadian goods, claiming the country cannot survive without the US.The White House announced the sweeping round of import duties – due to come into effect next month – on Monday, igniting fresh tensions between Washington and Ottawa. Continue reading...

items.title: Donald Trump to impose 50% tariff on most Canadian goods, White House says
items.standfirst: Turmoil likely as Trump officials say Canada unfairly discriminated against US autos, alcohol and dairy productsDonald Trump is imposing 50% tariffs on most Canadian goods in response to the country retaliating against previous US tariffs, the White House announced on Monday, declaring Canada has unfairly discriminated against American cars, alcohol and dairy products.The tariffs will hit a wide range of products, the White House said, including wine, hockey sticks and cement. They also include goods previously protected from import taxes under the United States-Mexico-Canada (USMCA) agreement. Continue reading...
items.content: Turmoil likely as Trump officials say Canada unfairly discriminated against US autos, alcohol and dairy productsDonald Trump is imposing 50% tariffs on most Canadian goods in response to the country retaliating against previous US tariffs, the White House announced on Monday, declaring Canada has unfairly discriminated against American cars, alcohol and dairy products.The tariffs will hit a wide range of products, the White House said, including wine, hockey sticks and cement. They also include goods previously protected from import taxes under the United States-Mexico-Canada (USMCA) agreement. Continue reading...

items.content: The wreckage of a Pan Am plane that crashed 74 years ago, and led to safety briefings becoming mandatory on flights, has been found off the coast of Puerto Rico.

items.title: British woman jailed in Hong Kong for blackmail after accusing banker of rape
items.standfirst: Isabel Rose, 26, sentenced to six years for blackmail and perverting course of public justice over her allegations against British manA British woman who reported being raped to police in Hong Kong has been sentenced to six years in prison for blackmail and perverting the course of public justice.Isabel Rose, 26, from south-east London, travelled to Hong Kong in early 2024 to visit a British banker she had met while travelling in south-east Asia. Continue reading...

clusters.title: India: Youth-led 'Cockroach' protests increase pressure on Modi's government
clusters.neutral_headline: India: Youth-led 'Cockroach' protests increase pressure on Modi's government
bullet_summary: Reported by France24, Guardian World

items.title: India: Youth-led 'Cockroach' protests increase pressure on Modi's government
items.standfirst: With each passing day, India's youth-led cockroach movement seems to swell. As the sit-in by anti-corruption campaigners grows in New Delhi, opposition lawmakers relay their call for the education minister to resign over the leaking of exam tests. The standoff grew with the Supreme Court refusing to hear a police brutality petition launched after Monday's crackdown on those trying to reach parliament. Details from the ground by FRANCE 24 correspondent in New Delhi, Sarah Jacob.
items.content: With each passing day, India's youth-led cockroach movement seems to swell. As the sit-in by anti-corruption campaigners grows in New Delhi, opposition lawmakers relay their call for the education minister to resign over the leaking of exam tests. The standoff grew with the Supreme Court refusing to hear a police brutality petition launched after Monday's crackdown on those trying to reach parliament. Details from the ground by FRANCE 24 correspondent in New Delhi, Sarah Jacob.

items.title: Opposition parties shut down Indian parliament in solidarity with ‘cockroach’ protests
items.standfirst: Proceedings suspended as politicians call for Modi government to address police violence and education scandalsIndia’s opposition parties have shut down parliament in a protest calling for the resignation of the education minister, in solidarity with the new youth-led “cockroach” movement.Opposition politicians, led by the Congress party leader Rahul Gandhi, gathered outside the parliament building in Delhi on Wednesday, many dressed in black and waving placards calling on Dharmendra Pradhan to quit. Continue reading...
